name: Macrosphenidae
description: Crombecs and African warblers are a diverse family of small passerine birds endemic to sub-Saharan Africa. They vary greatly in size and bill shape, reflecting their varied insectivorous foraging strategies.

children.name: Sylvietta
children.description: A genus of small, short-tailed warblers with long curved bills, found in woodlands across sub-Saharan Africa.

children.name: Sylvietta ruficapilla
children.description: The Red-capped Crombec is a small warbler with a distinctive rufous cap and pale underparts. It inhabits miombo woodlands in central and southern Africa, gleaning insects from branches.

children.name: Sylvietta rufescens
children.description: The Long-billed Crombec is a grey-brown warbler with a notably long, slender bill and a very short tail. It is common in savanna and scrub habitats across southern Africa.

children.name: Melocichla
children.description: A monotypic genus of large grass warbler with a distinctive black moustachial stripe.

children.name: Melocichla mentalis
children.description: The Moustached Grass Warbler is a large, streaky brown warbler with a bold black moustachial stripe. It inhabits tall grasslands and reeds across sub-Saharan Africa.

children.name: Sphenoeacus
children.description: A monotypic genus of large grass warbler endemic to southern Africa.

children.name: Sphenoeacus afer
children.description: The Cape Grassbird is a large, rufous-crowned warbler with a long tail and streaked upperparts. It is found in fynbos and grassland habitats in South Africa and Eswatini.
